perturbation.original_text: PROGRAM as used herein means an exploratory exchange and evaluation of technical and business information among the Parties pertaining to the EFL System.
perturbation.changed_text: PROJECT as used herein means an exploratory exchange and evaluation of technical and business information among the Parties pertaining to the EFL System.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'PROGRAM' to 'PROJECT' creates confusion. The agreement defines 'PROGRAM', but now the definition refers to 'PROJECT'. All other mentions of program might not be applicable as the agreement clearly defines 'PROGRAM'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROJECT as used herein means an exploratory exchange and evaluation of technical and business information among the Parties pertaining to the EFL System.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'PROGRAM'. The changed text redefines 'PROGRAM' as 'PROJECT'. The contradicted text also defines 'PROJECT'. This creates a contradiction because the agreement now has two conflicting definitions for the same term, or uses PROGRAM and PROJECT interchangably without defining them, violating the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: PROPRIETARY INFORMATION - any information disclosed by one PARTY to the respective other PARTY, which is transferred: (a) by means of documents including, but not limited to, agreements, drawings or pictures, designated as confidential at the time of disclosure and marked accordingly; (b) any specimen, physical item or any other object, whether it be marked "confidential" or not; (c) visual or verbal disclosure which is subsequently contained in a document marked "confidential" and delivered to the receiving PARTY within 60 days from its disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: CONFIDENTIAL DATA - any information disclosed by one PARTY to the respective other PARTY, which is transferred: (a) by means of documents including, but not limited to, agreements, drawings or pictures, designated as confidential at the time of disclosure and marked accordingly; (b) any specimen, physical item or any other object, whether it be marked "confidential" or not; (c) visual or verbal disclosure which is subsequently contained in a document marked "confidential" and delivered to the receiving PARTY within 60 days from its disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION' to 'CONFIDENTIAL DATA' causes an internal contradiction, because the contract refers only to 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION' later. The main issue arises from the fact that all later clauses use the term 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION,' leading to ambiguity about whether 'CONFIDENTIAL DATA' is afforded the same protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION Each Party agrees to protect PROPRIETARY INFORMATION of the other Party, not to use proprietary information for any purpose other than stated in the PROGRAM OBJECTIVE and not to disclose PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to any third party. The Parties shall each secure 1 <PAGE> in writing a similar non-disclosure agreement from each of its employees prior to any disclosure of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to that person. IEC shall employ in general the same standard of care as it employs with respect to its own proprietary or confidential information of like kind and character. There shall be no obligation with respect to PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which: a) is or becomes publicly available without fault of the receiving Party; b) can be shown by competent written proof to have been in the possession of the receiving Party as of the date of the disclosure; c) is disclosed without restriction to the receiving Party by a third party having the legal right to make such disclosures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION' with 'CONFIDENTIAL DATA'. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION' and outlines obligations related to its non-disclosure. Because the contract now uses the term 'CONFIDENTIAL DATA' but does not explicitly state it is subject to the same protections as 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION', a contradiction exists. This creates ambiguity about whether 'CONFIDENTIAL DATA' is afforded the same protection, thus impacting the enforceability of the non-disclosure agreement as it pertains to the newly defined term. Therefore, this satisfies the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: The objective of the PROGRAM is to provide a mechanism for the sharing of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which will enable IEC to evaluate the benefits of the EFL System.
perturbation.changed_text: The aim of the PROJECT is to provide a mechanism for the sharing of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which will enable IEC to evaluate the benefits of the EFL System.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'objective' to 'aim'. The contract uses both the words 'objective' and 'aim' making it ambiguous if there are 2 different things
perturbation.contradicted_text: The aim of the PROJECT is to provide a mechanism for the sharing of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which will enable IEC to evaluate the benefits of the EFL System.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation indicates that the contract uses both the words 'objective' and 'aim'. The original text uses 'objective' and the changed text swaps it to 'aim'. The contradicted text also uses the word 'aim'. The explanation states the change causes ambiguity about whether there are two different things being referenced, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the misaligned terminology definition. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction.
